title: Adapter Problem Json Errors
description: SpecD Studio capability **Adapter Problem Json Errors** (`client:adapter-problem-json-errors`). Translate HTTP problem responses into typed errors the UI can display. This spec is canonical after archive: implementers rely on the requirements below, not change-only documents.
context: SpecD Studio capability **Adapter Problem Json Errors** (`client:adapter-problem-json-errors`). Translate HTTP problem responses into typed errors the UI can display. This spec is canonical after archive: implementers rely on the requirements below, not change-only documents.

rules: When response `Content-Type` is `application/problem+json`, the adapter MUST parse `status`, `title`, `detail`, and specd-specific extension fields into a throwable error type.
For `ArtifactConflictError` (HTTP 409), the adapter MUST preserve enough detail for `ui:hooks-inspector-save` to show a conflict UI.

constraints: @specd/ui and @specd/client MUST NOT import @specd/core or @specd/sdk for kernel bootstrap.
HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.name: Problem body becomes SpecdClientError
then: AND specd code extension is preserved

scenarios.name: Non-problem HTML is not mislabeled
given: reverse proxy returns HTML error page
then: AND parser does not throw parse exception

scenarios.name: 409 retains conflict metadata
then: error type is recognizable by save hook
AND detail includes hash conflict context

scenarios.name: Inspector can show conflict UI
then: UI offers keep local or reload server
